source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting, EDS Activation

event.top_event: Unauthorized discharge of Zinc Bromide due to Emergency Disconnect Sequence activation
actions_taken: Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS) initiated from drill floor panel
Drillship transited 30 meters south to safe zone
Visual deck checks for damage performed
DP and Thruster Control System verification scheduled by manufacturer
event.summary: On January 15, 2022, at approximately 16:10 hours, the drillship Noble Stanley Lafosse experienced a Dynamic Position (DP) system failure due to misalignment of Thruster-2 (180 degrees out of alignment) during completion operations at Green Canyon Block 389. High winds and sudden wind direction changes caused the misaligned thruster to push the drillship in the same direction as the wind, compromising DP position into red alert status. The Emergency Disconnect Sequence was initiated, unlatching the Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP) from the Blow Out Preventers (BOP). This resulted in an unauthorized discharge of approximately 420-558 barrels of 15.1 PPG Zinc Bromide (ZnBr2) into the Gulf of Mexico. The incident was contributed to by the operator's failure to complete the submitted Well Suspension Procedure in a safe and timely manner despite knowing inclement weather was approaching.
recommendations: Operator to send letter of explanation to Houma District Office addressing INC G-110 and detailing prevention measures for future incidents

hazards.name: High wind and adverse weather conditions
Pressurized riser containing Zinc Bromide fluid

threats.name: Thruster misalignment causing loss of DP position control
Rig drift into red alert zone due to compromised DP
Incomplete well suspension procedure during weather event

consequences.name: Discharge of Zinc Bromide into Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Dynamic Positioning System
controls.barrier_role: Maintain rig position during adverse weather
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent rig drift

supporting_text: the drillship's DP position was compromised into a red alert status due to high winds and a misaligned thruster
Thruster-2 is located on the port bow and was found to be 180 degrees out of alignment
When the DP system increased the thrust on Thruster-2, it increased but in the opposite direction, thus going in the same direction as the wind

controls.name: Thruster-2 alignment and verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure thruster operates in correct direction to maintain DP

supporting_text: Thruster-2 is located on the port bow and was found to be 180 degrees out of alignment
the operator has corrected the misalignment of Thruster-2 and will perform a DP and Thruster Control System verification by the manufacturer

controls.name: Well Suspension Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe and timely well suspension before weather event
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent unplanned EDS

supporting_text: the operator failed to complete the submitted Well Suspension Procedure in a safe and timely manner knowing inclement weather was approaching
At 15:00 hours, the drill crew was instructed by the operator to stop TIH in the hole, and at 15:30 hours, to start POOH after a discussion with their management in town
This resulted in an EDS instead of a planned and safe disconnect of the LMRP

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS)
controls.barrier_role: Safely disconnect LMRP and isolate BOP when rig position is compromised
controls.lod_basis: Fail-safe system to prevent loss of well control

supporting_text: The EDS sequence was initiated from the drill floor panel and the LMRP was unlatched from the Blow Out Preventers (BOP)
All the BOP fail safe valves, UBSR, and LBSR were confirmed closed
the rig transited 30 meters to the south of the well location, arriving in the safe zone area at approximately 16:29 hours

controls.name: Blind Shear Rams (UBSR and LBSR) closure and testing
controls.barrier_role: Isolate wellbore and prevent fluid discharge during EDS

supporting_text: All the BOP fail safe valves, UBSR, and LBSR were confirmed closed
the LBSR moved to the block position as per EDS sequence initiation

controls.name: Pre-Job Safety Meeting
controls.barrier_role: Communicate hazards and procedures before critical operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure crew awareness

supporting_text: After holding a Pre-Job Safety Meeting, the lower blind shear rams (LBSR) were closed to test the LBSR

controls.name: BSEE approval for well suspension procedure
controls.barrier_role: Regulatory oversight of well suspension operations during adverse weather

supporting_text: On January 15, 2022, at 00:07 hours, the operator received verbal approval from the BSEE Houma District to prepare the wellbore as requested for a weather event
At 14:21 hours, the operator received verbal approval to proceed with running the storm packer as requested and retest the blind shear rams

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
